notes:
v2.0.0 Update
🎬 Cinematic Effects: Add one of 13 short particle or impact effects to a personal cut-in or All-Out Attack — debris, sparks, embers, confetti, petals, sparkles, lens rain, shockwave, hero flare, film burn, blackout cut, speed lines, or shadow mist. Each has Low/Medium/High strength, shows in the live preview, and works alongside Screen Mood. No image assets or WebGL, and a client setting lets each player skip effects on low-end machines.

🌙 Screen Moods: Nine screen filters — Night, Dusk, Dawn, Flashback, Vignette, Dream, Old TV, Lightning, Monochrome Film — with an opacity slider, on both personal cut-ins and All-Out Attacks.

🎨 Blank Theme: A bare-stage theme with no panels or borders, for building your own look from custom layers.

🖱️ Direct Preview Editing: Drag character, text, and layers in the preview to reposition them; Ctrl + mouse wheel adjusts scale or text size.

🔔 Notifications: Update notices now use the shared GlitchSmith Library board, so this release needs GlitchSmith Library v0.9.0 or newer.

announcement.title: 🎬 Cinematic Cut-ins v2.0.0
announcement.content:
v2.0.0: Cinematic Effects, Screen Moods & Preview Editing

  • Cinematic Effects: Layer one of 13 built-in effects over a personal cut-in or All-Out Attack (debris, sparks, embers, confetti, petals, sparkles, lens rain, shockwave, hero flare, film burn, blackout cut, speed lines, shadow mist), each with Low/Medium/High strength and a live preview. Built from CSS and generated shapes, so no image assets or WebGL. A per-player setting can skip them on low-end machines.
  • Screen Moods: Nine screen filters (Night, Dusk, Dawn, Flashback, Vignette, Dream, Old TV, Lightning, Monochrome Film) with an opacity slider, on personal cut-ins and All-Out Attacks.
  • Blank Theme: A minimal no-graphics theme for building a custom look from layers.
  • Direct Preview Editing: Drag character, text, and layers in the preview to reposition; Ctrl + mouse wheel changes scale or text size.
  • Requires GlitchSmith Library v0.9.0+: Update notices now go through the shared GlitchSmith Library board instead of the module's own dialog.
